title: pinus cembra subsp. pumila
intro: Pinus cembra subsp. pumila, also known as the dwarf Swiss stone pine, is a slow-growing evergreen shrub that belongs to the Pinaceae family. The resin of Pinus cembra subsp. pumila has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The resin has been reported to exhibit health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and fighting off infections. The resin can be prepared into herbal products such as ointments, salves, and tinctures, which are often used to apply topically to the affected area.
